problem:
create_agent with ProviderStrategy binds empty tools list and breaks OpenAI-compatible providers. When using `create_agent(..., response_format=MySchema)` without any user tools, LangChain can still end up binding an empty tool list in the `ProviderStrategy` path.

I expect LangChain to omit tool binding entirely in that case, since provider-native structured output does not require tool calling when there are no tools.

Instead, the request path reaches `model.bind_tools([])`, which causes fail (npm config set registry is not working — it seems correct per old docs)
memory_hint: Use npmrc for registry config. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using npm config set registry which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
